subject: Prepare for java 11
commit_message:
Prepare for java 11

final changes for java11 groovy compatibility
removed unnecessary dependencies and cleaned up poms
added missing jaxws-api dependency to pom.xml
java8 profile is now active by default in parent pom
update beans to have RemoteResource annotation
always use the compiler plugin from parent pom
Update external task timeout to longer value to hold lock
update remote resource annotation to match catalog db client
add remote resource annotation to bean for bowman
completely remove bowman from requests db client

Issue-ID: SO-2059
Signed-off-by: Benjamin, Max (mb388a) <mb388a@att.com>
Change-Id: If6f0df36e8077ccd703abce9f64290daed63955b

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

we should wait for gate to be working again,
we also should use ONAP Java11 version

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> we should wait for gate to be working again,
> we also should use ONAP Java11 version

The idea here on this review is to "prepare" the code base without requiring the infrastructure to switch to java11. There is a java11 profile which will switch the compile version. We have tested this in AT&T on openjdk 11.0.6 and java8. All of these changes are to make sure that we're using dependencies which support java 11.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> > 
> > we should wait for gate to be working again,
> > we also should use ONAP Java11 version
> 
> The idea here on this review is to "prepare" the code base without requiring the infrastructure to switch to java11. There is a java11 profile which will switch the compile version. We have tested this in AT&T on openjdk 11.0.6 and java8. All of these changes are to make sure that we're using dependencies which support java 11.

Not that not against the change you propose (I'm actually a lot in favor).

I just saw that just so-simulator image is changed, my bad, I thought it was for the whole images.

Nevertheless, using ONAP base java image from integration on so-simulator and validating this (huge) change with gating seems to be the way to go, at least for me.

Patch Set 9:

Hello, 

I believe it's really infortunate not to try to understand why gating failed here.
You also didn't gave a chance to have a technical discussion on why this test has failed (knowing it never fails usually...) by commenting and merging in the same minute.

I also understand that gating is a waste of resource here. And as these resources are constrained (we're holding CLAMP to use them for example), I prefer not to waste them here and use them for project with real will to use it.

I am happy to have a discussion about why gating failed but I am not provided enough information to act upon it. Where are the logs from the robot run? Where is the source code for the tests? Can you point me to a wiki for these?

I have no issue troubleshooting your CI for weeks (https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-2941) but as discussed master is not stable or gating for SO. Gate SO on Frankfurt, Dublin, Casablanca, Berlin, Amsterdam, where there's an expectation of stability.

> I am happy to have a discussion about why gating failed but I am not provided enough information to act upon it. Where are the logs from the robot run? Where is the source code for the tests? Can you point me to a wiki for these?

On any gating attempt, you get an test results url in gerrit
in your case the link was https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//107820-9/index.html
it is no more available as you merged (logs are cleaned once the patch is merged)
but you can find some examples
the link shall lead you to a page like this one: https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_frankfurt-605055463-06-22-2020_02-06/
if you click on the different test components, you can have access to all the logs corresponding to the tests
- onap-k8s => https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_frankfurt-605055463-06-22-2020_02-06/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/index.html
here you can even have access at the log of each pod (at the end of the deployment)
- healtchcheck: https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_frankfurt-605055463-06-22-2020_02-06/healthcheck/full/full/report.html
- healthdist (onboarding/distribution of the vFW) - test that was broken by the change: https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_frankfurt-605055463-06-22-2020_02-06/healthcheck/healthdist/healthdist/report.html
- end to end onboarding/distribution/instantiation: https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_frankfurt-605055463-06-22-2020_02-06/smoke-usecases/basic_vm/xtesting.debug.log
....

several presentations have been done since we put this system in place in February 2019 (DDF, PTL meetings, TSC meetings, Integration meetings)
An Integration Q&A is available here: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/OOM+Gating
It is also part of the official ONAP Integration documentation: https://onap-doc.readthedocs.io/projects/onap-integration/en/frankfurt/onap-integration-ci.html#integration-ci

The code for the healthcheck is the one you can find in the onap/testsuite repository

> 
> I have no issue troubleshooting your CI for weeks (https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-2941) but as discussed master is not stable or gating for SO.
This is our next release and there may be some issues in the interim. I must have missed the vote when we agreed to allow this CI process to gate SO on master. Gate SO on Frankfurt, Dublin, Casablanca, Berlin, Amsterdam, where there's an expectation of stability.

As far as I remember, it was decided to add Gating to SO with Seshu during the ONS or the DDF.
Several projects asked to get such feature, due to the fact that we did not have enough resources (full ONAP deployment on each patchset submission), it was decided to priviledge OOM (installation) and SO (critical core component)

As Gating is a tool to give feedback to developers by deploying an OnDemand ONAP per patch. It rebuilds the docker then deploy and test. You have access to the logs of lots of tests dealing with infrastructure, component healthcheck, end to end tests and security tests. 
Gating Master is a usual process in open source projets. Master does not mean necessarily unstable. It is a dev branch, feature can be incomplete but there is no reason in this case for instance that the change prevents the vFW to be distributed..it maybe reports a regression or an interesting side effect that is always better to fix before any merge.

If you look at the last daily Master over the last 7 days, it is pretty stable (look at the end https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/)

Thank you for assuming I was allowed to attend these. Can more information be included with each gating link to link back to these overview pages for those new to this whole system? This explanation was really helpful and I now understand more. I think other contributors not present for these meetings would benefit from this information being included with failures.

I had no idea I could get the pod logs from k8s on https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_frankfurt-605055463-06-22-2020_02-06/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/index.html

Can I also get the pod logs from the links written back to the pull request? Like this one? https://gating-results.onap.eu/results/onap_daily_pod4_frankfurt-605055463-06-22-2020_02-06/

The robot logs are sort of helpful, but do not provide enough information to debug. 

The error message I saw had to do with a connectivity failure. How do I retrigger the check on a future code review?

Is SO still failing on master after this change was merged? If it is I'm happy to look at it. If not, then my original interpretation was correct.

Yep it is a bad point for Seshu 😊
PTL have to report such things to their team..
SO was the first "component" project so it would probably have made sense to plan a x-session Integration/SO to explain that. It is still possible to do..

> The error message I saw had to do with a connectivity failure. How do I retrigger the check on a future code review?

the gating is automatically trigger on any new patchset
there is a queuing mechanism
so if you submit 5 in a row, we will probably consider only the last one.
But each new version of the patchset will trigger a new attempt
and in case something wrong happen (we may have infrastructure issue), there is a magic word in gerrit to force the redeployment

> 
> Is SO still failing on master after this change was merged? If it is I'm happy to look at it. If not, then my original interpretation was correct.

There will be 2 ways to see that
1) if SO self releases new docker including this patch in OOM
2) on subsequent SO patch if gating is still active but I think it is disabled now

Thank you Morgan for clearing things up. Searching for magic words, I can now see the new one added oom_redploy. Perhaps Sylvain misunderstood my comments in the other thread. I don't have an issue with the automation running on master, I have an issue with it expecting to prevent a merge there.

I don't see others having an issue merging while chained fails on master.
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/so/+/109303

Or on Frankfurt:
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/so/+/108598

It is unclear to me why this review in particular caused such an uproar.

Let's shift our conversation to the point that these CI runs do not stop anyone from merging on any branch currently. While that is the policy I think it's wrong to single out particular pulls and claim they are in violation. 

If these "gating results" are a merge requirement, then enforce them with no exception (just as with the unit tests and compilation). Otherwise, we make decisions to merge things and will eventually fix our failing CI or image builds.

If I've learned anything in this discussion it's that there are those here who prefer to demonstrate their power over all the projects and contributors by revoking features and holding changes hostage.

I do not know if that's a community I want to be a part of.
